info.title: ElevenLabs API Documentation
info.description: This is the documentation for the ElevenLabs API. You can use this API to use our service programmatically, this is done by using your API key. You can find your API key in the dashboard at https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys.

get.description: Returns a list of your generated audio (e.g. text to speech, speech to speech, Studio, dubbing). Music and SFX generations are not included and cannot currently be retrieved via the API.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: How many history items to return at maximum. Can not exceed 1000, defaults to 100.

schema.title: Page Size
schema.description: How many history items to return at maximum. Can not exceed 1000, defaults to 100.

parameters.name: start_after_history_item_id
parameters.description: After which ID to start fetching, use this parameter to paginate across a large collection of history items. In case this parameter is not provided history items will be fetched starting from the most recently created one ordered descending by their creation date.

schema.title: Start After History Item Id
schema.description: After which ID to start fetching, use this parameter to paginate across a large collection of history items. In case this parameter is not provided history items will be fetched starting from the most recently created one ordered descending by their creation date.

parameters.name: voice_id
parameters.description: Voice ID to be filtered for, you can use GET https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/voices to receive a list of voices and their IDs.

schema.title: Voice Id
schema.description: Voice ID to be filtered for, you can use GET https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/voices to receive a list of voices and their IDs.

parameters.name: model_id
parameters.description: Model ID to filter history items by.

schema.title: Model Id
schema.description: Model ID to filter history items by.

parameters.name: date_before_unix
parameters.description: Unix timestamp to filter history items before this date (exclusive).

schema.title: Date Before Unix
schema.description: Unix timestamp to filter history items before this date (exclusive).

parameters.name: date_after_unix
parameters.description: Unix timestamp to filter history items after this date (inclusive).

schema.title: Date After Unix
schema.description: Unix timestamp to filter history items after this date (inclusive).

parameters.name: sort_direction
parameters.description: Sort direction for the results.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: search term used for filtering

schema.title: Search
schema.description: search term used for filtering

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source of the generated history item

parameters.name: xi-api-key
parameters.description: Your API key. This is required by most endpoints to access our API programmatically. You can view your xi-api-key using the 'Profile' tab on the website.

schema.title: Xi-Api-Key
schema.description: Your API key. This is required by most endpoints to access our API programmatically. You can view your xi-api-key using the 'Profile' tab on the website.

post.description: Download one or more history items. If one history item ID is provided, we will return a single audio file. If more than one history item IDs are provided, we will provide the history items packed into a .zip file.

200.description: The requested audio file, or a zip file containing multiple audio files when multiple history items are requested.

example.message: output_format must be wav or none

post.description: Turn text into sound effects for your videos, voice-overs or video games using the most advanced sound effects models in the world.

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

parameters.name: enable_logging
parameters.description: When enable_logging is set to false zero retention mode will be used for the request. This will mean history features are unavailable for this request, including request stitching. Zero retention mode may only be used by enterprise customers.

schema.title: Enable request logging.
schema.description: When enable_logging is set to false zero retention mode will be used for the request. This will mean history features are unavailable for this request, including request stitching. Zero retention mode may only be used by enterprise customers.

parameters.name: optimize_streaming_latency
parameters.description:
You can turn on latency optimizations at some cost of quality. The best possible final latency varies by model. Possible values:
0 - default mode (no latency optimizations)
1 - normal latency optimizations (about 50% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
2 - strong latency optimizations (about 75% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
3 - max latency optimizations
4 - max latency optimizations, but also with text normalizer turned off for even more latency savings (best latency, but can mispronounce eg numbers and dates).

Defaults to None.

schema.title: DEPRECATED. How much we should optimize streaming request latency (time to first audio byte).
schema.description:
You can turn on latency optimizations at some cost of quality. The best possible final latency varies by model. Possible values:
0 - default mode (no latency optimizations)
1 - normal latency optimizations (about 50% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
2 - strong latency optimizations (about 75% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
3 - max latency optimizations
4 - max latency optimizations, but also with text normalizer turned off for even more latency savings (best latency, but can mispronounce eg numbers and dates).

Defaults to None.

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM and WAV formats with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

parameters.name: enable_logging
parameters.description: When enable_logging is set to false zero retention mode will be used for the request. This will mean history features are unavailable for this request, including request stitching. Zero retention mode may only be used by enterprise customers.

schema.title: Enable request logging.
schema.description: When enable_logging is set to false zero retention mode will be used for the request. This will mean history features are unavailable for this request, including request stitching. Zero retention mode may only be used by enterprise customers.

parameters.name: optimize_streaming_latency
parameters.description:
You can turn on latency optimizations at some cost of quality. The best possible final latency varies by model. Possible values:
0 - default mode (no latency optimizations)
1 - normal latency optimizations (about 50% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
2 - strong latency optimizations (about 75% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
3 - max latency optimizations
4 - max latency optimizations, but also with text normalizer turned off for even more latency savings (best latency, but can mispronounce eg numbers and dates).

Defaults to None.

schema.title: DEPRECATED. How much we should optimize streaming request latency (time to first audio byte).
schema.description:
You can turn on latency optimizations at some cost of quality. The best possible final latency varies by model. Possible values:
0 - default mode (no latency optimizations)
1 - normal latency optimizations (about 50% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
2 - strong latency optimizations (about 75% of possible latency improvement of option 3)
3 - max latency optimizations
4 - max latency optimizations, but also with text normalizer turned off for even more latency savings (best latency, but can mispronounce eg numbers and dates).

Defaults to None.

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM and WAV formats with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

schema.description: Which field to sort by, one of 'created_at_unix' or 'name'. 'created_at_unix' may not be available for older voices.

parameters.name: sort_direction

schema.title: Sort Direction
schema.description: Which direction to sort the voices in. 'asc' or 'desc'.

parameters.name: voice_type
parameters.description: Type of the voice to filter by. One of 'personal', 'community', 'default', 'workspace', 'non-default', 'non-community', 'saved'. 'non-default' is equal to all but 'default'. 'non-community' is equal to 'personal' and 'workspace' combined (excludes library copies). 'saved' is equal to non-default, but includes default voices if they have been added to a collection.

schema.title: Voice Type
schema.description: Type of the voice to filter by. One of 'personal', 'community', 'default', 'workspace', 'non-default', 'non-community', 'saved'. 'non-default' is equal to all but 'default'. 'non-community' is equal to 'personal' and 'workspace' combined (excludes library copies). 'saved' is equal to non-default, but includes default voices if they have been added to a collection.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category of the voice to filter by. One of 'premade', 'cloned', 'generated', 'professional'

schema.title: Category
schema.description: Category of the voice to filter by. One of 'premade', 'cloned', 'generated', 'professional'

parameters.name: fine_tuning_state
parameters.description: State of the voice's fine tuning to filter by. Applicable only to professional voices clones. One of 'draft', 'not_verified', 'not_started', 'queued', 'fine_tuning', 'fine_tuned', 'failed', 'delayed'

schema.title: Fine Tuning State
schema.description: State of the voice's fine tuning to filter by. Applicable only to professional voices clones. One of 'draft', 'not_verified', 'not_started', 'queued', 'fine_tuning', 'fine_tuned', 'failed', 'delayed'

parameters.name: collection_id
parameters.description: Collection ID to filter voices by.

schema.title: Collection Id
schema.description: Collection ID to filter voices by.

parameters.name: include_total_count
parameters.description: "Whether to include the total count of voices found in the response. NOTE: The total_count value is a live snapshot and may change between requests as users create, modify, or delete voices. For pagination, rely on the has_more flag instead. Only enable this when you actually need the total count (e.g., for display purposes), as it incurs a performance cost."

post.description: Transcribe an audio or video file. If webhook is set to true, the request will be processed asynchronously and results sent to configured webhooks. When use_multi_channel is true and the provided audio has multiple channels, a 'transcripts' object with separate transcripts for each channel is returned; set multichannel_output_style='combined' to instead receive a single transcript with all channels merged and sorted by time. Otherwise, returns a single transcript. The optional webhook_metadata parameter allows you to attach custom data that will be included in webhook responses for request correlation and tracking.

post.description: Separate an audio file into individual stems. This endpoint might have high latency, depending on the length of the audio file.

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

AgentTrustContext.title: AgentTrustContext
AgentTrustContext.description:
"The trust context in which the agent operates.

UNKNOWN: not yet classified (existing agents created before this feature).
LOW: serves untrusted external participants (e.g. customer support, sales) —
     outputs should be vetted and tool access scoped.
HIGH: serves the owner (e.g. personal assistant) — full tool access is appropriate."

AlertingSettings.title: AlertingSettings
AlertingSettings.description:
"Alerting configuration used at both per-agent and per-workspace level.

All fields are optional overrides; the cascade resolver fills in defaults
when they are unset. Notifiers stack and dedupe (by URL) across the
workspace and agent layers rather than overriding each other.

Cascade order for per-monitor threshold and auto-resolve: agent → workspace →
system default."

description.description: The description of the property. When set, the LLM will provide the value based on this description. Mutually exclusive with dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

is_system_provided.title: Is System Provided
is_system_provided.description: If true, the value will be populated by the system at runtime. Used by API Integration Webhook tools for templating. Mutually exclusive with description, dynamic_variable, constant_value, and is_omitted.

dynamic_variable.title: Dynamic Variable
dynamic_variable.description: The name of the dynamic variable to use for this property's value. Mutually exclusive with description, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

allowed_values_dynamic_variable.title: Allowed Values Dynamic Variable
allowed_values_dynamic_variable.description: When set, the LLM provides the value but the runtime rejects any value not present in the list held by this dynamic variable. Use to let the LLM pick from a server-verified set (e.g. the IDs the current user is allowed to access). Requires description; mutually exclusive with dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

is_omitted.title: Is Omitted
is_omitted.description: If true, this parameter will be completely omitted from the request. Only valid for optional parameters. Mutually exclusive with description, dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, and constant_value.

AnalysisProperty.description:
"Data collection property with optional per-item LLM override for post-call analysis.

inheritance, so this generalizes cleanly to object/array schemas in the future."

AuthConnectionStatus.title: AuthConnectionStatus
AuthConnectionStatus.description:
Single status field shared by every auth type's stored credential.

OAuth values (``REFRESH_FAILED``, ``REVOKED``) are written by the OAuth
token-manager refresh path. ``CREDENTIAL_INVALID`` is written by the
tool execution path when an upstream response matches a credential's
``failure_signatures`` entry (Bearer, Basic auth, etc.).

speculative_turn.title: Speculative Turn
speculative_turn.description: When enabled, starts generating LLM responses during silence before full turn confidence is reached, reducing perceived latency. May increase LLM costs.

retranscribe_on_turn_timeout.title: Retranscribe On Turn Timeout
retranscribe_on_turn_timeout.description: When enabled, if VAD detects no speech, attempts to re-transcribe accumulated audio at turn timeout. Disables silence discount billing for affected turns.

interruption_ignore_terms.title: Interruption Ignore Terms
interruption_ignore_terms.description: List of terms that should not trigger an interruption when spoken by the user (e.g. 'gotcha', 'understood'). Uses case-insensitive exact matching.

interruption_ignore_term_languages.title: Interruption Ignore Term Languages
interruption_ignore_term_languages.description: Language codes for which preset ignore-term categories have been activated. Stored explicitly so display is not inferred from term overlap.

transcribe_on_disabled_interruptions.title: Transcribe On Disabled Interruptions
transcribe_on_disabled_interruptions.description: When interruptions are disabled, still transcribe what the user says so it can carry into the next turn. When off, user speech during a non-interruptible turn is ignored and won't trigger a turn.

finetune_strength.description: How strongly the finetune influences the generation. Defaults to 1.0 (full strength). Lower values soften the influence of the finetune, leaving more room for prompt-level steering. Only meaningful when `finetune_id` is also provided.

use_phonetic_names.title: Use Phonetic Names
use_phonetic_names.description: If true, proper names in the prompt will be phonetically spelled in the lyrics for better pronunciation by the music model. The original names will be restored in word timestamps.

respect_sections_durations.title: Respect Sections Durations
respect_sections_durations.description: Controls how strictly section durations in the `composition_plan` are enforced. Only used with `composition_plan` and only applies to `music_v1`; for `music_v2` section durations are always enforced and this is ignored. When false for `music_v1`, the model may adjust individual section durations for better quality and latency, while preserving the total song duration from the plan.

store_for_inpainting.description: Whether to store the generated song for inpainting.

with_timestamps.title: With Timestamps

pronunciation_dictionary_locators.description: A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (pronunciation_dictionary_id, version_id) encoded as a list of JSON strings for pronunciation dictionaries to be applied to the text. A list of json encoded strings is required as adding projects may occur through formData as opposed to jsonBody. To specify multiple dictionaries use multiple --form lines in your curl, such as --form 'pronunciation_dictionary_locators="{\"pronunciation_dictionary_id\":\"Vmd4Zor6fplcA7WrINey\",\"version_id\":\"hRPaxjlTdR7wFMhV4w0b\"}"' --form 'pronunciation_dictionary_locators="{\"pronunciation_dictionary_id\":\"JzWtcGQMJ6bnlWwyMo7e\",\"version_id\":\"lbmwxiLu4q6txYxgdZqn\"}"'.

from_content_json.title: From Content Json
from_content_json.description:
"
    An optional content to initialize the Studio project with. If this is set, 'from_url' and 'from_document' must be null. If neither 'from_url', 'from_document', 'from_content' are provided we will initialize the Studio project as blank.

    Example:
    [{\"name\": \"Chapter A\", \"blocks\": [{\"sub_type\": \"p\", \"nodes\": [{\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT0\", \"text\": \"A\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}, {\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT1\", \"text\": \"B\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}]}, {\"sub_type\": \"h1\", \"nodes\": [{\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT0\", \"text\": \"C\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}, {\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT1\", \"text\": \"D\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}]}]}, {\"name\": \"Chapter B\", \"blocks\": [{\"sub_type\": \"p\", \"nodes\": [{\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT0\", \"text\": \"E\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}, {\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT1\", \"text\": \"F\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}]}, {\"sub_type\": \"h2\", \"nodes\": [{\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT0\", \"text\": \"G\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}, {\"voice_id\": \"6lCwbsX1yVjD49QmpkT1\", \"text\": \"H\", \"type\": \"tts_node\"}]}]}]
    "

drop_background_audio.title: Drop Background Audio
drop_background_audio.description: An advanced setting. Whether to drop background audio from the final dub. This can improve dub quality where it's known that audio shouldn't have a background track such as for speeches or monologues.

dubbing_studio.title: Dubbing Studio
dubbing_studio.description: Whether to prepare dub for edits in dubbing studio or edits as a dubbing resource.

disable_voice_cloning.title: Disable Voice Cloning
disable_voice_cloning.description: Instead of using a voice clone in dubbing, use a similar voice from the ElevenLabs Voice Library. Voices used from the library will contribute towards a workspace's custom voices limit, and if there aren't enough available slots the dub will fail. Using this feature requires the caller to have the 'add_voice_from_voice_library' permission on their workspace to access new voices.

remove_background_noise.description: If set will remove background noise for voice samples using our audio isolation model. If the samples do not include background noise, it can make the quality worse.

moderate_metadata.title: Moderate Metadata
moderate_metadata.description: Run synchronous LLM moderation over the voice name and description when they change. Has no effect unless the voice_library_metadata_moderation feature flag is enabled for the user.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: A simple text prompt to compose a plan from.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: Start of the time range as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Must be at least 2020-01-01.

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: End of the time range as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Must be at least 2020-01-01.

interval_seconds.title: Interval Seconds
interval_seconds.description: "Bucket size in seconds. Each row in the response covers this many seconds of the selected time range. For example, pass 3600 for hourly buckets or 86400 for daily buckets. Whether `time_zone` shifts bucket boundaries depends on this value: whole-day multiples (e.g. 86400) align to local midnight; whole-hour multiples up to 24 hours (e.g. 3600, 14400) align to local hour boundaries from midnight; sub-hour values and other sizes remain UTC-anchored regardless of `time_zone`."

time_zone.title: Time Zone
time_zone.description: IANA time zone identifier (e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'UTC') used to align bucket boundaries for eligible `interval_seconds` values. Whole-day multiples start at local midnight; whole-hour multiples up to 24 hours align to local hour boundaries from midnight. Sub-hour intervals and other bucket sizes remain UTC-anchored regardless of this setting. Defaults to UTC.

model_id.title: Model Id
model_id.description: Identifier of the model that will be used, you can query them using GET /v1/models. The model needs to have support for speech to speech, you can check this using the can_do_voice_conversion property.

remove_background_noise.title: Remove Background Noise
remove_background_noise.description: If set, will remove the background noise from your audio input using our audio isolation model. Only applies to Voice Changer.

tag_audio_events.title: Tag Audio Events
tag_audio_events.description: Whether to tag audio events like (laughter), (footsteps), etc. in the transcription.

diarize.title: Diarize
diarize.description: Whether to annotate which speaker is currently talking in the uploaded file.

webhook.title: Webhook
webhook.description: Whether to send the transcription result to configured speech-to-text webhooks.  If set the request will return early without the transcription, which will be delivered later via webhook.

use_multi_channel.title: Use Multi Channel
use_multi_channel.description: Whether the audio file contains multiple channels where each channel contains a single speaker. When enabled, each channel is transcribed independently. By default a separate transcript is returned per channel; set multichannel_output_style='combined' to instead receive a single transcript with all channels merged and sorted by time. Each word in the response includes a 'channel_index' field indicating which channel it was spoken on. A maximum of 5 channels is supported. Each channel is billed independently at the full audio duration, so cost scales linearly with the number of channels.

no_verbatim.title: No Verbatim
no_verbatim.description: If true, the transcription will not have any filler words, false starts and non-speech sounds. Only supported with scribe_v2 model.

use_speaker_library.title: Use Speaker Library
use_speaker_library.description: Whether to use the speaker library for identifying known speakers during diarization. When enabled and diarize is true, detected speakers will be matched against registered speakers in the workspace's speaker library.

detect_speaker_roles.title: Detect Speaker Roles
detect_speaker_roles.description: Whether to detect speaker roles (agent vs customer). Requires diarize=true. Cannot be used with use_multi_channel=true. When enabled, speaker_id values will be 'agent' and 'customer' instead of 'speaker_0', 'speaker_1', etc. Usage incurs an additional 10% surcharge on base transcription cost.

entity_redaction_mode.title: Entity Redaction Mode
entity_redaction_mode.description: How to format redacted entities. 'redacted' replaces with {REDACTED}, 'entity_type' replaces with {ENTITY_TYPE}, 'enumerated_entity_type' replaces with {ENTITY_TYPE_N} where N enumerates each occurrence. Only used when entity_redaction is set.

keyterms.title: Keyterms
keyterms.description: "A list of keyterms to bias the transcription towards.           The keyterms are words or phrases you want the model to recognise more accurately.           The number of keyterms cannot exceed 1000.           The length of each keyterm must be less than 50 characters.           Keyterms can contain at most 5 words (after normalisation).           For example [\"hello\", \"world\", \"technical term\"].           The following characters are not supported: `<`, `>`, `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `\`.           Usage of this parameter will incur an additional 20% surcharge on the base transcription cost.           When more than 100 keyterms are provided, a minimum billable duration of 20 seconds applies per request."

For reliable generation, keep the total character count across all `inputs[].text` values at or below 2,000 characters per request. Longer requests can terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error.

videos.title: Videos
videos.description:
One or more video files sent via FormData array (multipart/form-data). They will be combined into one codec in order.
            A maximum of 10 videos is allowed, where the total size of the combined video is limited to 200MB.
            In total, the video can be up to 600 seconds long. Note that combining multiple videos may increase the request duration significantly. If possible, combine the videos beforehand.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: A list of dialogue inputs, each containing text and a voice ID which will be converted into speech. The maximum number of unique voice IDs is 10. Longer requests can terminate early in streaming responses or return a validation error.

pronunciation_dictionary_locators.title: Pronunciation Dictionary Locators
pronunciation_dictionary_locators.description: A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (id, version_id) to be applied to the text. They will be applied in order. You may have up to 3 locators per request

previous_request_ids.title: Previous Request Ids
previous_request_ids.description: A list of request_id of the samples that were generated before this generation. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when splitting up a large task into multiple requests. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both previous_text and previous_request_ids is send, previous_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request_ids can be send.

next_request_ids.title: Next Request Ids
next_request_ids.description: A list of request_id of the samples that come after this generation. next_request_ids is especially useful for maintaining the speech's continuity when regenerating a sample that has had some audio quality issues. For example, if you have generated 3 speech clips, and you want to improve clip 2, passing the request id of clip 3 as a next_request_id (and that of clip 1 as a previous_request_id) will help maintain natural flow in the combined speech. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both next_text and next_request_ids is send, next_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request_ids can be send.

ConfigEntityType.title: ConfigEntityType
ConfigEntityType.description:
Entity types for the API configuration.

This enum contains all valid entity type configurations that users can specify:
- Parent types (e.g., "name", "financial_id") that expand to all subtypes
- Specific subtypes using dot notation (e.g., "name.full_name")
- Standalone terminal types (e.g., "email_address")

When converted for service use, parent types expand to all their terminal subtypes.

history_message_count.description: How much recent history the guardrail sees before the reply it evaluates, counted in user messages (the agent replies between them are included too). The guardrail always gets a single <conversation_history> transcript ending in the evaluated reply, marked 'AGENT [current reply]:'. 0 (default) adds no prior history (just that line); 1 adds the latest user message onward.

history_include_tool_calls.title: History Include Tool Calls
history_include_tool_calls.description: "When enabled, the history also renders the agent's tool calls, their input arguments, and the tool results, interleaved with the text, so an action between two agent messages is visible. Off by default: history shows only user and agent text. Tool payloads can be large and can mislead smaller evaluator models, so enabling this raises token cost."

DeleteCalendarEventParams.title: DeleteCalendarEventParams
DeleteCalendarEventParams.description:
Permanently remove a previously-cancelled calendar event.

This delete tool is the irreversible follow-up to cancel_calendar_event.
The backend rejects the call (422) if the event hasn't been
cancelled yet, so the only safe path is cancel-then-delete.

DeleteGroupSessionParams.title: DeleteGroupSessionParams
DeleteGroupSessionParams.description:
"Permanently remove a previously-cancelled group session.

Group analogue of ``delete_calendar_event``: cancel
(``cancel_group_session_for_all``) is the soft, history-preserving step;
this tool is the irreversible follow-up that drops the row from Mongo
and the staff Google Calendar entirely. The backend rejects the call
(422) if the session hasn't been cancelled yet, so the only safe path
is cancel-then-delete."

text.title: Text
text.description: The text config to be generated for this chunk. Can contain section name in square brackets, e.g. [Verse 1], lyrics lines, and inline directions in curly braces, e.g. {scratching}.

duration_ms.title: Duration Ms
duration_ms.description: The duration of the chunk in milliseconds. Must be between 3000ms and 120000ms.

positive_styles.title: Positive Styles
positive_styles.description: The styles and musical directions that should be present in this chunk. Use English language for best results. The styles for the first chunk are the most important as they set the overall tone and genre. Styles for subsequent chunks can be used to add nuance, progression, emphasis, or change the direction of the song. Aim to have at least 6-7 styles in early chunks until the direction is established. Generic styles like 'great production quality' are good default styles to append to the list.

negative_styles.title: Negative Styles
negative_styles.description: The styles and musical directions that should not be present in this chunk. Use English language for best results. Leaving empty is a good default, only use this field if you want to explicitly avoid a particular style or direction.

GetAppointmentByConfirmationNumberParams.title: GetAppointmentByConfirmationNumberParams
GetAppointmentByConfirmationNumberParams.description:
Look up an appointment by the booking confirmation number the caller quotes.

The confirmation number is the 8-character code shown on the booking
confirmation page (e.g. ``#01ABCDEF``). Callers may read it back with or
without the leading ``#`` and with varied spacing; the tool normalizes
the input and does a prefix match on the stored calendar item id.

ListCustomerFacingAgentsParams.title: ListCustomerFacingAgentsParams
ListCustomerFacingAgentsParams.description:
"List every customer-facing agent on the workspace.

The assistant uses this whenever it needs to act on a specific customer-facing
agent (rules, config edits, etc.) so it can pick the right ``agent_id`` to pass
to mutating tools. Mirrors the ``list_services`` / ``list_clients``
pattern: read once, then mutate by id."

ListGroupSessionsParams.title: ListGroupSessionsParams
ListGroupSessionsParams.description:
List scheduled group sessions for a group service in a date range.

Group services are scheduled in advance (e.g. yoga classes, workshops) and
callers register against an existing session. Use this for group services;
use ``check_service_availability`` for appointment / rental services.

LiteralJsonSchemaProperty.title: LiteralJsonSchemaProperty

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the property. When set, the LLM will provide the value based on this description. Mutually exclusive with dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

is_system_provided.title: Is System Provided
is_system_provided.description: If true, the value will be populated by the system at runtime. Used by API Integration Webhook tools for templating. Mutually exclusive with description, dynamic_variable, constant_value, and is_omitted.

dynamic_variable.title: Dynamic Variable
dynamic_variable.description: The name of the dynamic variable to use for this property's value. Mutually exclusive with description, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

allowed_values_dynamic_variable.title: Allowed Values Dynamic Variable
allowed_values_dynamic_variable.description: When set, the LLM provides the value but the runtime rejects any value not present in the list held by this dynamic variable. Use to let the LLM pick from a server-verified set (e.g. the IDs the current user is allowed to access). Requires description; mutually exclusive with dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, constant_value, and is_omitted.

is_omitted.title: Is Omitted
is_omitted.description: If true, this parameter will be completely omitted from the request. Only valid for optional parameters. Mutually exclusive with description, dynamic_variable, is_system_provided, and constant_value.

LiteralJsonSchemaProperty.description: "Schema property for literal JSON types. IMPORTANT: Only ONE of the following fields can be set: description (LLM provides value), dynamic_variable (value from variable), is_system_provided (system provides value), constant_value (fixed value), or is_omitted (parameter is omitted). These are mutually exclusive."

SkipTurnToolConfig.title: SkipTurnToolConfig
SkipTurnToolConfig.description:
"Allows the agent to explicitly skip its turn.

This tool should be invoked by the LLM when the user indicates they would like
to think or take a short pause before continuing the conversation—e.g. when
they say: \"Give me a second\", \"Let me think\", or \"One moment please\".  After
calling this tool, the assistant should not speak until the user speaks
again, or another normal turn-taking condition is met.  The tool itself has
no parameters and performs no side-effects other than informing the backend
that the current turn generation is complete."

additional_soft_timeout_messages.title: Additional Soft Timeout Messages
additional_soft_timeout_messages.description: Extra static filler messages for subsequent soft timeouts in the same LLM generation. The first timeout uses `message`. If fewer messages are configured than `max_soft_timeouts_per_generation`, the last configured message is repeated; otherwise a built-in filler is used.

use_llm_generated_message.title: Use Llm Generated Message
use_llm_generated_message.description: If enabled, the soft timeout message will be generated dynamically instead of using the static message.

randomize_fillers.title: Randomize Fillers
randomize_fillers.description: If enabled, shuffle the order of static soft timeout messages once at the start of each turn. Only applies when use_llm_generated_message is false.

max_soft_timeouts_per_generation.title: Max Soft Timeouts Per Generation
max_soft_timeouts_per_generation.description: Maximum filler messages while waiting for a single LLM response. Fires every timeout_seconds until the LLM streams content or this limit is reached.

UuiTransferConfig.title: UUITransferConfig

data.title: Data
data.description: UUI payload to send on SIP REFER transfers. Supports inline dynamic variables and is hex-encoded at transfer time.

UuiTransferConfig.description:
User-to-User Information envelope for SIP REFER transfers (RFC 7433).

Outbound payloads are hex-encoded (the only encoding RFC 7433 defines). The
protocol discriminator axis lets per-platform formats (Talkdesk, Genesys, ...)
be expressed by configuration rather than scattered transfer flags. Further
axes (ASCII encoding, header name, purpose/content parameters) can be added
here without touching the transfer model.

VoicemailDetectionToolConfig.title: VoicemailDetectionToolConfig
VoicemailDetectionToolConfig.description:
Allows the agent to detect when a voicemail system is encountered.

This tool should be invoked by the LLM when it detects that the call has been
answered by a voicemail system rather than a human. If a voicemail message
is configured, it will be played; otherwise the call will end immediately.

BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Callback Url
BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description:
"
    A url that will be called by our service when the Studio project is converted. Request will contain a json blob containing the status of the conversion
    Messages:
    1. When project was converted successfully:
    {
      type: \"project_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        conversion_status: \"success\",
        project_snapshot_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        error_details: None,
      }
    }
    2. When project conversion failed:
    {
      type: \"project_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        conversion_status: \"error\",
        project_snapshot_id: None,
        error_details: \"Error details if conversion failed\"
      }
    }

    3. When chapter was converted successfully:
    {
      type: \"chapter_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        chapter_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        conversion_status: \"success\",
        chapter_snapshot_id: \"23m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAV\",
        error_details: None,
      }
    }
    4. When chapter conversion failed:
    {
      type: \"chapter_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        chapter_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        conversion_status: \"error\",
        chapter_snapshot_id: None,
        error_details: \"Error details if conversion failed\"
      }
    }
    "

BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Fiction
BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description: An optional specification of whether the content of this Studio project is fiction.

BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Apply Text Normalization
BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description:
"
    This parameter controls text normalization with four modes: 'auto', 'on', 'apply_english' and 'off'.
    When set to 'auto', the system will automatically decide whether to apply text normalization
    (e.g., spelling out numbers). With 'on', text normalization will always be applied, while
    with 'off', it will be skipped. 'apply_english' is the same as 'on' but will assume that text is in English.
    "

BodyCreateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description: If true, creates a corresponding read for direct publishing in draft state

BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.title: Voice Stability
BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.description: For models that support it, the voice similarity value to use. This will default to 0.65, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.title: Voice Similarity
BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.description: For models that support it, the voice similarity value to use. This will default to 1.0, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.title: Voice Style
BodyCreateANewSpeakerV1DubbingResourceDu.description: For models that support it, the voice style value to use. This will default to 1.0, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyCreateChapterV1StudioProjectsProject.description: An optional URL from which we will extract content to initialize the Studio project. If this is set, 'from_url' and 'from_content' must be null. If neither 'from_url', 'from_document', 'from_content' are provided we will initialize the Studio project as blank.

BodyCreateFileDocumentV1ConvaiKnowledgeB.title: Name
BodyCreateFileDocumentV1ConvaiKnowledgeB.description: A custom, human-readable name for the document.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostMod.description: The type of podcast to generate. Can be 'conversation', an interaction between two voices, or 'bulletin', a monologue.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostSou.title: Source
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostSou.description: The source content for the Podcast.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostLan.title: Language
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostLan.description: An optional language of the Studio project. Two-letter language code (ISO 639-1).

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostInt.title: Intro
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostInt.description: The intro text that will always be added to the beginning of the podcast.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostOut.title: Outro
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostOut.description: The outro text that will always be added to the end of the podcast.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostIns.title: Instructions Prompt
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostIns.description: Additional instructions prompt for the podcast generation used to adjust the podcast's style and tone.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostHig.title: Highlights
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostHig.description: A brief summary or highlights of the Studio project's content, providing key points or themes. This should be between 10 and 70 characters.

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostCal.title: Callback Url
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostCal.description:
"
    A url that will be called by our service when the Studio project is converted. Request will contain a json blob containing the status of the conversion
    Messages:
    1. When project was converted successfully:
    {
      type: \"project_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        conversion_status: \"success\",
        project_snapshot_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        error_details: None,
      }
    }
    2. When project conversion failed:
    {
      type: \"project_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        conversion_status: \"error\",
        project_snapshot_id: None,
        error_details: \"Error details if conversion failed\"
      }
    }

    3. When chapter was converted successfully:
    {
      type: \"chapter_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        chapter_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        conversion_status: \"success\",
        chapter_snapshot_id: \"23m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAV\",
        error_details: None,
      }
    }
    4. When chapter conversion failed:
    {
      type: \"chapter_conversion_status\",
      event_timestamp: 1234567890,
      data: {
        request_id: \"1234567890\",
        project_id: \"21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM\",
        chapter_id: \"22m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikMAT\",
        conversion_status: \"error\",
        chapter_snapshot_id: None,
        error_details: \"Error details if conversion failed\"
      }
    }
    "

BodyDubAVideoOrAnAudioFileV1DubbingPostC.description: Frames per second to use when parsing a CSV file for dubbing. If not provided, FPS will be inferred from timecodes.

BodyGenerateCompositionPlanV1MusicPlanPo.description: The length of the composition plan to generate in milliseconds. Must be between 3000ms and 600000ms. Optional - if not provided, the model will choose a length based on the prompt.

BodyGenerateCompositionPlanV1MusicPlanPo.title: Source Composition Plan
BodyGenerateCompositionPlanV1MusicPlanPo.description: An optional composition plan to use as a source for the new composition plan.

BodyGetSimilarLibraryVoicesV1SimilarVoic.title: Similarity Threshold
BodyGetSimilarLibraryVoicesV1SimilarVoic.description: Threshold for voice similarity between provided sample and library voices. Values range from 0 to 2. The smaller the value the more similar voices will be returned.

BodyGetSimilarLibraryVoicesV1SimilarVoic.title: Top K
BodyGetSimilarLibraryVoicesV1SimilarVoic.description: Number of most similar voices to return. If similarity_threshold is provided, less than this number of voices may be returned. Values range from 1 to 100.

BodyHandleAnOutboundCallViaTwilioV1Conva.description: Whether let Twilio record the call.

BodyInviteUserV1WorkspaceInvitesAddPostW.description: The workspace permission of the user. This is deprecated, use `seat_type` instead.

BodyInviteUserV1WorkspaceInvitesAddPostG.description: The group ids of the user

BodyInviteUserV1WorkspaceInvitesAddPostU.description: Monthly credit usage limit for the invitee. Omit or set to null for no custom cap.

BodyListApiRequestsV1WorkspaceAnalyticsR.description: Start of the time range as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

BodyListApiRequestsV1WorkspaceAnalyticsR.title: End Time
BodyListApiRequestsV1WorkspaceAnalyticsR.description: End of the time range as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

BodyListApiRequestsV1WorkspaceAnalyticsR.title: Sort
BodyListApiRequestsV1WorkspaceAnalyticsR.description: Optional timestamp sort direction. If omitted, defaults to desc when end_time is provided, otherwise asc.

BodyMoveEntityToFolderV1ConvaiKnowledgeB.title: Move To
BodyMoveEntityToFolderV1ConvaiKnowledgeB.description: The folder to move the entities to. If not set, the entities will be moved to the root folder.

BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.title: Platform Settings
BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.description: Platform settings for the agent are all settings that aren't related to the conversation orchestration and content.

BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.title: Name
BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.description: A name to make the agent easier to find

BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.title: Tags
BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.description: Tags to help classify and filter the agent

BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.title: Version Description
BodyPatchesAnAgentSettingsV1ConvaiAgents.description: Description for this version when publishing changes (only applicable for versioned agents)

BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.title: Media
BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.description: The media file to upload. Mutually exclusive with media_url.

BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.title: Media Url
BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.description: A URL to fetch the media file from. Mutually exclusive with media.

BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.title: Media Url Filename
BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.description: The filename for URL-sourced media (e.g. 'example.mp4'). Required when using media_url.

BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.title: Media Url Content Type
BodyRegisterMediaV1ProductionsOrdersOrde.description: The MIME type for URL-sourced media (e.g. 'video/mp4'). Required when using media_url.

BodyRenderAudioOrVideoForTheGivenLanguag.title: Normalize Volume
BodyRenderAudioOrVideoForTheGivenLanguag.description: Whether to normalize the volume of the rendered audio.

BodyRequestManualVerificationV1VoicesPvc.title: Extra Text
BodyRequestManualVerificationV1VoicesPvc.description: Extra text to be used in the manual verification process.

BodyRunPvcTrainingV1VoicesPvcVoiceIdTrai.title: Model Id
BodyRunPvcTrainingV1VoicesPvcVoiceIdTrai.description: The model ID to use for the conversion.

BodySetWorkspaceThirdPartyDisablingPolic.title: Third Party Disable Allowed
BodySetWorkspaceThirdPartyDisablingPolic.description: `true` forces every key in the workspace to be disable-able by its holder; `false` forbids it for every key; `null` clears the override (per-key values and the plan default apply).

BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.title: User Email
BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.description: The email of the user or service account.

BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.title: Group Id
BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.description: The ID of the target group. Use 'default' to set the resource's baseline role — every workspace member receives this role unless they hold a higher one through a direct user grant, group membership, or workspace (service account) API key.

BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.title: Workspace Api Key Id
BodyShareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceRes.description: The ID of the target workspace (service account) API key. This is not the API key string itself that you pass in the header for authentication — it is the key's ID, which workspace admins can find under Developers → Service Accounts.

BodySimulatesAConversationStreamV1Convai.title: Extra Evaluation Criteria
BodySimulatesAConversationStreamV1Convai.description: A list of evaluation criteria to test

BodySimulatesAConversationV1ConvaiAgents.title: Extra Evaluation Criteria
BodySimulatesAConversationV1ConvaiAgents.description: A list of evaluation criteria to test

BodySoundGenerationV1SoundGenerationPost.title: Duration Seconds
BodySoundGenerationV1SoundGenerationPost.description: The duration of the sound which will be generated in seconds. Must be at least 0.5 and at most 30. If set to None we will guess the optimal duration using the prompt. Defaults to None.

BodySoundGenerationV1SoundGenerationPost.title: Prompt Influence
BodySoundGenerationV1SoundGenerationPost.description: A higher prompt influence makes your generation follow the prompt more closely while also making generations less variable. Must be a value between 0 and 1. Defaults to 0.3.

BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.title: Voice Settings
BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.description: Voice settings overriding stored settings for the given voice. They are applied only on the given request. Needs to be send as a JSON encoded string.

BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.title: Seed
BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.title: File Format
BodySpeechToSpeechStreamingV1SpeechToSpe.description: The format of input audio. Options are 'pcm_s16le_16' or 'other' For `pcm_s16le_16`, the input audio must be 16-bit PCM at a 16kHz sample rate, single channel (mono), and little-endian byte order. Latency will be lower than with passing an encoded waveform.

BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.title: Voice Settings
BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.description: Voice settings overriding stored settings for the given voice. They are applied only on the given request. Needs to be send as a JSON encoded string.

BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.title: Seed
BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.title: File Format
BodySpeechToSpeechV1SpeechToSpeechVoiceI.description: The format of input audio. Options are 'pcm_s16le_16' or 'other' For `pcm_s16le_16`, the input audio must be 16-bit PCM at a 16kHz sample rate, single channel (mono), and little-endian byte order. Latency will be lower than with passing an encoded waveform.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostFile.title: File
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostFile.description: The file to transcribe (100ms minimum audio length). All major audio and video formats are supported. Exactly one of the file or cloud_storage_url parameters must be provided. The file size must be less than 5.0GB.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostLangua.title: Language Code
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostLangua.description: An ISO-639-1 or ISO-639-3 language_code corresponding to the language of the audio file. Can sometimes improve transcription performance if known beforehand. Defaults to null, in this case the language is predicted automatically.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostNumSpe.title: Num Speakers
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostNumSpe.description: The maximum amount of speakers talking in the uploaded file. Can help with predicting who speaks when. The maximum amount of speakers that can be predicted is 32. Defaults to null, in this case the amount of speakers is set to the maximum value the model supports.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostDiariz.title: Diarization Threshold
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostDiariz.description: Diarization threshold to apply during speaker diarization. A higher value means there will be a lower chance of one speaker being diarized as two different speakers but also a higher chance of two different speakers being diarized as one speaker (less total speakers predicted). A low value means there will be a higher chance of one speaker being diarized as two different speakers but also a lower chance of two different speakers being diarized as one speaker (more total speakers predicted). Can only be set when diarize=True and num_speakers=None. Defaults to None, in which case we will choose a threshold based on the model_id (0.22 usually).

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostCloudS.title: Cloud Storage Url
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostCloudS.description: [Deprecated] This parameter is deprecated and will be removed in the future. Use 'source_url' instead.The HTTPS URL of the file to transcribe. Exactly one of the file or cloud_storage_url parameters must be provided. The file must be accessible via HTTPS and the file size must be less than 2GB. Any valid HTTPS URL is accepted, including URLs from cloud storage providers (AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage, Cloudflare R2, etc.), CDNs, or any other HTTPS source. URLs can be pre-signed or include authentication tokens in query parameters.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostSource.title: Source Url
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostSource.description: The URL of an audio or video file to transcribe. Supports hosted video or audio files, YouTube video URLs, TikTok video URLs, and other video hosting services.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostWebhoo.title: Webhook Id
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostWebhoo.description: Optional specific webhook ID to send the transcription result to. Only valid when webhook is set to true. If not provided, transcription will be sent to all configured speech-to-text webhooks.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostTemper.title: Temperature
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostTemper.description: Controls the randomness of the transcription output. Accepts values between 0.0 and 2.0, where higher values result in more diverse and less deterministic results. If omitted, we will use a temperature based on the model you selected which is usually 0.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostSeed.title: Seed
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostSeed.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be an integer between 0 and 2147483647.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostWebhoo.title: Webhook Metadata
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostWebhoo.description: Optional metadata to be included in the webhook response. This should be a JSON string representing an object with a maximum depth of 2 levels and maximum size of 16KB. Useful for tracking internal IDs, job references, or other contextual information.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostEntity.title: Entity Detection
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostEntity.description: Detect entities in the transcript. Can be 'all' to detect all entities, a single entity type or category string, or a list of entity types/categories. Categories include 'pii', 'phi', 'pci', 'other', 'offensive_language'. When enabled, detected entities will be returned in the 'entities' field with their text, type, and character positions. Usage of this parameter will incur an additional 30% surcharge on the base transcription cost.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostEntity.title: Entity Redaction
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostEntity.description: "Redact entities from the transcript text. Accepts the same format as entity_detection: 'all', a category ('pii', 'phi'), or specific entity types. Must be a subset of entity_detection. When redaction is enabled, the entities field will not be returned. Usage of this parameter will incur an additional 30% surcharge on the base transcription cost."

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Prompt
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: A simple text prompt to generate a song from. Cannot be used in conjunction with `composition_plan`.
Optional generation mode hint for prompt-based music generation. Can only be used with `prompt`.
A music prompt. Deprecated. Use `composition_plan` instead.

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Lyrics Text
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: The lyrics text to use for the generation.

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Composition Plan
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: A detailed composition plan to guide music generation. Cannot be used in conjunction with `prompt`.

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Music Length Ms
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: The length of the song to generate in milliseconds. Used only in conjunction with `prompt`. Must be between 3000ms and 600000ms. Optional - if not provided, the model will choose a length based on the prompt.

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Seed
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: Random seed to initialize the music generation process. Providing the same seed with the same parameters can help achieve more consistent results, but exact reproducibility is not guaranteed and outputs may change across system updates. Cannot be used in conjunction with prompt.

BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.title: Finetune Id
BodyStreamComposedMusicV1MusicStreamPost.description: The ID of the finetune to use for the generation

BodySubmitABatchCallRequestV1ConvaiBatch.description: Maximum number of simultaneous calls for this batch. When set, dispatch is governed by this limit rather than workspace/agent capacity percentages.

BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.title: Language Code
BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.description: Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model and text normalization. If the model does not support the provided language code, it will be ignored. This parameter is not supported for multilingual_v2 models.

BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.title: Pronunciation Dictionary Locators
BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.description: A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (id, version_id) to be applied to the text. They will be applied in order. You may have up to 3 locators per request

BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.title: Seed
BodyTextToDialogueMultiVoiceStreamingV1T.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

BodyUnshareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceR.title: Group Id
BodyUnshareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceR.description: The ID of the target group. Use 'default' to set the resource's baseline role — every workspace member receives this role unless they hold a higher one through a direct user grant, group membership, or workspace (service account) API key.

BodyUnshareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceR.title: Workspace Api Key Id
BodyUnshareWorkspaceResourceV1WorkspaceR.description: The ID of the target workspace (service account) API key. This is not the API key string itself that you pass in the header for authentication — it is the key's ID, which workspace admins can find under Developers → Service Accounts.

BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.title: Selected Speaker Ids
BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.description: Speaker IDs to be used for PVC training. Make sure you send all the speaker IDs you want to use for PVC training in one request because the last request will override the previous ones.

BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.title: Trim Start Time
BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.description: The start time of the audio to be used for PVC training. Time should be in milliseconds

BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.title: Trim End Time
BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.description: The end time of the audio to be used for PVC training. Time should be in milliseconds

BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.title: File Name
BodyUpdatePvcVoiceSampleV1VoicesPvcVoice.description: The name of the audio file to be used for PVC training.

BodyUpdateStudioProjectContentV1StudioPr.title: From Url
BodyUpdateStudioProjectContentV1StudioPr.description: An optional URL from which we will extract content to initialize the Studio project. If this is set, 'from_url' and 'from_content' must be null. If neither 'from_url', 'from_document', 'from_content' are provided we will initialize the Studio project as blank.

BodyUpdateStudioProjectContentV1StudioPr.title: From Document
BodyUpdateStudioProjectContentV1StudioPr.description: An optional .epub, .pdf, .txt or similar file can be provided. If provided, we will initialize the Studio project with its content. If this is set, 'from_url' and 'from_content' must be null. If neither 'from_url', 'from_document', 'from_content' are provided we will initialize the Studio project as blank.

BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Title
BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description: An optional name of the author of the Studio project, this will be added as metadata to the mp3 file on Studio project or chapter download.

BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Author
BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description: An optional name of the author of the Studio project, this will be added as metadata to the mp3 file on Studio project or chapter download.

BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.title: Isbn Number
BodyUpdateStudioProjectV1StudioProjectsP.description: An optional ISBN number of the Studio project you want to create, this will be added as metadata to the mp3 file on Studio project or chapter download.

BodyUpdateAgentBranchV1ConvaiAgentsAgent.title: Name
BodyUpdateAgentBranchV1ConvaiAgentsAgent.description: New name for the branch. Must be unique within the agent.

BodyUpdateAgentBranchV1ConvaiAgentsAgent.title: Is Archived
BodyUpdateAgentBranchV1ConvaiAgentsAgent.description: Whether the branch should be archived
The protection level for the branch

BodyUpdateAudioNativeContentFromUrlV1Aud.title: Author
BodyUpdateAudioNativeContentFromUrlV1Aud.description: Author used in the player and inserted at the start of the uploaded article. If not provided, the default author set in the Player settings is used.

BodyUpdateAudioNativeContentFromUrlV1Aud.title: Title
BodyUpdateAudioNativeContentFromUrlV1Aud.description: Title used in the player and inserted at the top of the uploaded article. If not provided, the default title set in the Player settings is used.

BodyUpdateChapterV1StudioProjectsProject.description: The name of the chapter, used for identification only.

BodyUpdateMemberV1WorkspaceMembersPostIs.description: Whether to lock or unlock the user account.

BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.description: Either the identifier of a voice from the ElevenLabs voice library, or one of ['track-clone', 'clip-clone'].

BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.title: Voice Stability
BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.description: For models that support it, the voice similarity value to use. This will default to 0.65, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.title: Voice Similarity
BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.description: For models that support it, the voice similarity value to use. This will default to 1.0, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.title: Voice Style
BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.description: For models that support it, the voice style value to use. This will default to 1.0, with a valid range of [0.0, 1.0].

BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.title: Languages
BodyUpdateMetadataForASpeakerV1DubbingRe.description: Languages to apply these changes to. If empty, will apply to all languages.

BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.title: Retry Enabled
BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.description: Whether to enable automatic retries for transient failures (5xx, 429, timeout)

BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.title: Request Headers
BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.description: A list of request headers to include with the webhook delivery (optional)

BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.title: Events
BodyUpdateWorkspaceWebhookV1WorkspaceWeb.description: The complete set of workspace-level events this webhook should be subscribed to. The webhook is added to the events in the list and removed from any not in the list. Omit to leave the current event subscriptions unchanged.

BodyUploadMusicV1MusicUploadPostExtractC.title: Extract Composition Plan
BodyUploadMusicV1MusicUploadPostExtractC.description: Whether to generate and return the composition plan for the uploaded song. Pass a model id (`music_v1` or `music_v2`) to control which composition plan format is returned. Passing `true`/`false` is deprecated; `true` defaults to the `music_v1` plan format. Enabling this will increase the latency.

BodyVideoToMusicV1MusicVideoToMusicPostD.title: Description
BodyVideoToMusicV1MusicVideoToMusicPostD.description: Optional text description of the music you want. A maximum of 1000 characters is allowed.

BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.title: Permissions
BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.description: The permissions of the XI API.

BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.title: Character Limit
BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.description: The character limit of the XI API key. If provided this will limit the usage of this api key to n characters per month where n is the chosen value. Requests that incur charges will fail after reaching this monthly limit.

BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.title: Allowed Ips
BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.description: List of IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed to use this API key. Each entry may be a CIDR range (e.g. '10.0.0.0/24') or a bare IP address (normalized to /32 or /128). On create, omit or pass null to allow all IPs. On update, omit to leave the allowlist unchanged, or pass "clear" to remove it.

BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.title: Third Party Disable Allowed
BodyCreateServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceA.description: Whether the holder of this key may disable it via the self-disable endpoint. On create, omit or pass null to use the workspace's default (enabled for non-Enterprise plans, disabled for Enterprise plans). On update, omit to leave it unchanged, or pass "clear" to reset it to the workspace default. Only honored for workspaces with self-disable access enabled.

BodyCreateServiceAccountV1ServiceAccount.title: Default Sharing Groups
BodyCreateServiceAccountV1ServiceAccount.description: List of groups with their permission levels to share with by default. Each entry should specify a group_id and a permission_level (admin, editor, or viewer).

BodyEditServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceAcc.title: Is Enabled
BodyEditServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceAcc.description: Whether to enable or disable the API key.

BodyEditServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceAcc.title: Name
BodyEditServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceAcc.description: The name of the XI API key to use (used for identification purposes only).

BodyEditServiceAccountApiKeyV1ServiceAcc.description: The permissions of the XI API.

BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsLang.title: Language Code
BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsLang.description: Language code (ISO 639-1) used to enforce a language for the model and text normalization. If the model does not support the provided language code, it will be ignored. This parameter is not supported for multilingual_v2 models.

BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsPron.title: Pronunciation Dictionary Locators
BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsPron.description: A list of pronunciation dictionary locators (id, version_id) to be applied to the text. They will be applied in order. You may have up to 3 locators per request

BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsSeed.title: Seed
BodyTextToDialogueFullWithTimestampsSeed.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

BodyTextToSpeechFullSeed.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed. Must be integer between 0 and 4294967295.

BodyTextToSpeechFullPreviousText.title: Previous Text
BodyTextToSpeechFullPreviousText.description: The text that came before the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

BodyTextToSpeechFullNextText.title: Next Text
BodyTextToSpeechFullNextText.description: The text that comes after the text of the current request. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when concatenating together multiple generations or to influence the speech's continuity in the current generation.

BodyTextToSpeechFullPreviousRequestIds.title: Previous Request Ids
BodyTextToSpeechFullPreviousRequestIds.description: A list of request_id of the samples that were generated before this generation. Can be used to improve the speech's continuity when splitting up a large task into multiple requests. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both previous_text and previous_request_ids is send, previous_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request_ids can be send.

BodyTextToSpeechFullNextRequestIds.title: Next Request Ids
BodyTextToSpeechFullNextRequestIds.description: A list of request_id of the samples that come after this generation. next_request_ids is especially useful for maintaining the speech's continuity when regenerating a sample that has had some audio quality issues. For example, if you have generated 3 speech clips, and you want to improve clip 2, passing the request id of clip 3 as a next_request_id (and that of clip 1 as a previous_request_id) will help maintain natural flow in the combined speech. The results will be best when the same model is used across the generations. In case both next_text and next_request_ids is send, next_text will be ignored. A maximum of 3 request_ids can be send.

PromptAgentApiModelInputTimezone.title: Timezone
PromptAgentApiModelInputTimezone.description: Timezone for displaying current time in system prompt. If set, the current time will be included in the system prompt using this timezone. Must be a valid timezone name (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'UTC'). Recommended for accurate time-aware responses; without this, the agent has no knowledge of the current date/time unless you provide it via dynamic variables or tools, which can lead to incorrect or hallucinated time references.

PromptAgentApiModelWorkflowOverrideOutpu.description: Timezone for displaying current time in system prompt. If set, the current time will be included in the system prompt using this timezone. Must be a valid timezone name (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'UTC'). Recommended for accurate time-aware responses; without this, the agent has no knowledge of the current date/time unless you provide it via dynamic variables or tools, which can lead to incorrect or hallucinated time references.

ResourceAccessInfoAnonymousAccessLevelOv.title: Anonymous Access Level Override

ResourceAccessInfoAccessSource.title: Access Source
ResourceAccessInfoAccessSource.description: Why the requesting user has access to this resource. 'creator' = caller is the owner. 'explicit' = caller (or one of their workspace groups) is listed in role_to_group_ids beyond the workspace-wide everyone group. 'workspace_default' = the workspace-wide everyone group is listed in role_to_group_ids (every non-anon workspace member, including admins, sees this resource). 'workspace_admin' = caller is a workspace admin and the admin seat is the *only* path to access; reserved for docs nobody else can see. Lets the UI disclose why an admin-bypass viewer sees a doc that wasn't explicitly shared with them.

TurnConfigWorkflowOverrideRetranscribeOn.description: When enabled, if VAD detects no speech, attempts to re-transcribe accumulated audio at turn timeout. Disables silence discount billing for affected turns.

TurnConfigWorkflowOverrideTranscribeOnDi.description: When interruptions are disabled, still transcribe what the user says so it can carry into the next turn. When off, user speech during a non-interruptible turn is ignored and won't trigger a turn.

UpdateToolCallUnitTestRequestParentFolde.description: The ID of the parent folder. If not provided, the test will be moved to the root level.

VoiceDesignRequestModelText.title: Text
VoiceDesignRequestModelText.description: Text to generate, text length has to be between 100 and 1000.

VoiceDesignRequestModelSeed.title: Seed
VoiceDesignRequestModelSeed.description: Random number that controls the voice generation. Same seed with same inputs produces same voice.

VoiceDesignRequestModelRemixingSessionIt.title: Remixing Session Iteration Id
VoiceDesignRequestModelRemixingSessionIt.description: The id of the remixing session iteration where these generations should be attached to. If not provided, a new iteration will be created.

VoiceDesignRequestModelQuality.title: Quality
VoiceDesignRequestModelQuality.description: Higher quality results in better voice output but less variety.

VoiceDesignRequestModelReferenceAudioBas.title: Reference Audio Base64
VoiceDesignRequestModelReferenceAudioBas.description: Reference audio to use for the voice generation. The audio should be base64 encoded. Only supported when using the  eleven_ttv_v3 model.

VoiceDesignRequestModelPromptStrength.title: Prompt Strength
VoiceDesignRequestModelPromptStrength.description: Controls the balance of prompt versus reference audio when generating voice samples. 0 means almost no prompt influence, 1 means almost no reference audio influence. Only supported when using the eleven_ttv_v3 model.

VoicePreviewsRequestModelText.title: Text
VoicePreviewsRequestModelText.description: Text to generate, text length has to be between 100 and 1000.

VoicePreviewsRequestModelSeed.title: Seed
VoicePreviewsRequestModelSeed.description: Random number that controls the voice generation. Same seed with same inputs produces same voice.

VoiceRemixRequestModelText.title: Text
VoiceRemixRequestModelText.description: Text to generate, text length has to be between 100 and 1000.

VoiceRemixRequestModelSeed.title: Seed
VoiceRemixRequestModelSeed.description: Random number that controls the voice generation. Same seed with same inputs produces same voice.

VoiceRemixRequestModelRemixingSessionIte.title: Remixing Session Iteration Id
VoiceRemixRequestModelRemixingSessionIte.description: The id of the remixing session iteration where these generations should be attached to. If not provided, a new iteration will be created.

VoiceRemixRequestModelPromptStrength.title: Prompt Strength
VoiceRemixRequestModelPromptStrength.description: Controls the balance of prompt versus reference audio when generating voice samples. 0 means almost no prompt influence, 1 means almost no reference audio influence. Only supported when using the eleven_ttv_v3 model.
