VoiceResponseModelIsOwner.title: Is Owner
VoiceResponseModelIsOwner.description: Whether the voice is owned by the user.

VoiceResponseModelFavoritedAtUnix.title: Favorited At Unix
VoiceResponseModelFavoritedAtUnix.description: Timestamp when the voice was marked as favorite in Unix time.

VoiceResponseModelCreatedAtUnix.title: Created At Unix
VoiceResponseModelCreatedAtUnix.description: The creation time of the voice in Unix time.

VoiceResponseModelIsBookmarked.title: Is Bookmarked
VoiceResponseModelIsBookmarked.description: Whether the voice is bookmarked by the current user. Only relevant for community (library-copied) voices.

VoiceResponseModelRecordingQuality.title: Recording Quality
VoiceResponseModelRecordingQuality.description: The recording quality of the voice as determined by the review pipeline.

VoiceResponseModelLabellingStatus.title: Labelling Status
VoiceResponseModelLabellingStatus.description: The review pipeline status of the voice.

VoiceResponseModelRecordingQualityReason.title: Recording Quality Reason
VoiceResponseModelRecordingQualityReason.description: The reason for the recording quality assessment, as determined by the review pipeline.

VoiceSamplePreviewResponseModelDurationS.title: Duration Secs
VoiceSamplePreviewResponseModelDurationS.description: The duration of the audio in seconds.

VoiceSettingsResponseModelStability.title: Stability
VoiceSettingsResponseModelStability.description: Determines how stable the voice is and the randomness between each generation. Lower values introduce broader emotional range for the voice. Higher values can result in a monotonous voice with limited emotion.

VoiceSettingsResponseModelUseSpeakerBoos.title: Use Speaker Boost
VoiceSettingsResponseModelUseSpeakerBoos.description: This setting boosts the similarity to the original speaker. Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

VoiceSettingsResponseModelSimilarityBoos.title: Similarity Boost
VoiceSettingsResponseModelSimilarityBoos.description: Determines how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

VoiceSettingsResponseModelStyle.title: Style
VoiceSettingsResponseModelStyle.description: Determines the style exaggeration of the voice. This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker. It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.

VoiceSettingsResponseModelSpeed.title: Speed
VoiceSettingsResponseModelSpeed.description: Adjusts the speed of the voice. A value of 1.0 is the default speed, while values less than 1.0 slow down the speech, and values greater than 1.0 speed it up.

VoiceSharingModerationCheckResponseModel.title: Date Checked Unix

BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostDur.title: The duration of the generated podcast. This varies depending on the format, voice and language.
BodyCreatePodcastV1StudioPodcastsPostDur.description:
Duration of the generated podcast. Must be one of:
short - produces podcasts shorter than 3 minutes.
default - produces podcasts roughly between 3-7 minutes.
long - produces podcasts longer than 7 minutes.

BodyDubAVideoOrAnAudioFileV1DubbingPostM.title: Mode
BodyDubAVideoOrAnAudioFileV1DubbingPostM.description: The mode in which to run this Dubbing job. Defaults to automatic, use manual if specifically providing a CSV transcript to use. Note that manual mode is experimental and production use is strongly discouraged.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostTimest.title: Timestamps Granularity
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostTimest.description: The granularity of the timestamps in the transcription. 'word' provides word-level timestamps and 'character' provides character-level timestamps per word.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostFileFo.title: File Format
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostFileFo.description: The format of input audio. Options are 'pcm_s16le_16' or 'other' For `pcm_s16le_16`, the input audio must be 16-bit PCM at a 16kHz sample rate, single channel (mono), and little-endian byte order. Latency will be lower than with passing an encoded waveform.

BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostMultic.title: Multichannel Output Style
BodySpeechToTextV1SpeechToTextPostMultic.description: Controls the response shape when use_multi_channel is enabled. 'separate' (default) returns one transcript per channel under 'transcripts'. 'combined' merges all channels into a single transcript whose words are sorted by start time, each carrying a 'channel_index' - matching the single-channel response shape. 'combined' requires timestamps (timestamps_granularity must not be 'none') and does not support entity detection or redaction.

TextToSpeechFullOutputFormatParameter.title: Output format of the generated audio.
TextToSpeechFullOutputFormatParameter.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM and WAV formats with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

PCM and WAV formats with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

TextToSpeechStreamWithTimestampsOutputFo.title: Output format of the generated audio.
TextToSpeechStreamWithTimestampsOutputFo.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

TextToDialogueOutputFormatParameter.title: Output format of the generated audio.
TextToDialogueOutputFormatParameter.description: Output format of the generated audio. Formatted as codec_sample_rate_bitrate. So an mp3 with 22.05kHz sample rate at 32kbs is represented as mp3_22050_32. MP3 with 192kbps bitrate requires you to be subscribed to Creator tier or above. PCM and WAV formats with 44.1kHz sample rate requires you to be subscribed to Pro tier or above. Note that the μ-law format (sometimes written mu-law, often approximated as u-law) is commonly used for Twilio audio inputs.

ListDubsDubbingStatusParameter.title: Dubbing Status
ListDubsDubbingStatusParameter.description: What state the dub is currently in.

ListDubsFilterByCreatorParameter.title: Filter By Creator
ListDubsFilterByCreatorParameter.description: Filters who created the resources being listed, whether it was the user running the request or someone else that shared the resource with them.

ListDubsOrderByParameter.title: Order By
ListDubsOrderByParameter.description: The field to use for ordering results from this query.

ListDubsOrderDirectionParameter.title: Order Direction
ListDubsOrderDirectionParameter.description: The order direction to use for results from this query.

GetDubbingTranscriptsFormatTypeParameter.title: Format Type
GetDubbingTranscriptsFormatTypeParameter.description: Format to return transcript in. For subtitles use either 'srt' or 'webvtt', and for a full transcript use 'json'. The 'json' format is not yet supported for Dubbing Studio.

GetPronunciationDictionariesMetadataSort.title: Sort
GetPronunciationDictionariesMetadataSort.description: Which field to sort by, one of 'created_at_unix' or 'name'.

ListChatResponseTestsRouteSortModeParame.title: Sort Mode
ListChatResponseTestsRouteSortModeParame.description: Sort mode for listing tests. Use 'folders_first' to place folders before tests.

GetConversationHistoriesRouteSummaryMode.title: Summary Mode
GetConversationHistoriesRouteSummaryMode.description: Whether to include transcript summaries in the response.

GetConversationHistoryRouteFormatParamet.title: Format
GetConversationHistoryRouteFormatParamet.description: Response format. Defaults to 'json'. Set to 'opentelemetry' for an OTLP-compatible trace payload using the same structure as the post-call webhook.

TextSearchConversationMessagesRouteSumma.title: Summary Mode
TextSearchConversationMessagesRouteSumma.description: Whether to include transcript summaries in the response.

tags.name: text-to-speech
tags.description: Convert text into lifelike speech using a voice of your choice.

tags.name: speech-to-speech
tags.description: Create speech by combining the style and content of an audio file you upload with a voice of your choice.

tags.name: speech-to-text
tags.description: Transcribe your audio files with detailed speaker annotations and precise timestamps using our cutting-edge model.

tags.name: forced-alignment
tags.description: Force align an audio file to a text transcript to get precise word-level and character level timing information. Response is a list of characters with their start and end times as milliseconds elapsed from the start of the recording.

tags.name: productions

tags.name: voices
tags.description: Access to voices created either by you or ElevenLabs.

tags.name: samples
tags.description: Access to your samples. A sample is any audio file you attached to a voice. A voice can have one or more samples.

tags.name: speech-history
tags.description: Accesses your speech history. Your speech history is a list of all your created audio including its metadata using our text-to-speech and speech-to-speech models.

tags.name: studios
tags.description: Access, create and convert Studio Projects programmatically, only specifically whitelisted accounts can access the Studio API. If you need access please contact our sales team.

tags.name: text-to-dialogue
tags.description: Generate multi-speaker dialogue from a script using different voices.

tags.name: sound-generation
tags.description: Generate sound effects and non-speech audio from a text prompt.

tags.name: audio-isolation
tags.description: Isolate speech from background noise in an audio file.

tags.name: audio-native
tags.description: Embed AI-narrated audio players into your website using Audio Native.

tags.name: music-generation
tags.description: Generate music from a text prompt.

tags.name: video-to-music
tags.description: Generate music that matches the content and mood of a video.

tags.name: studio
tags.description: Access, create and convert Studio Projects programmatically. Only specifically whitelisted accounts can access the Studio API. If you need access please contact our sales team.
